hero.label: Made for Prelim
hero.headline: I want to drive growth for the banking infrastructure that matters.
hero.subheadline: Prelim is solving a real problem for financial institutions: making digital account opening frictionless at scale. After closing 15-20 paying clients as a BDR and running B2B digital campaigns at NextAfter, I'm ready to own growth strategy for a product that banks actually need.

whyCompany.title: Banking digitalization is massive, unsexy, and urgent
whyCompany.body: Most banks still run legacy systems. Prelim's no-code platform removes friction from account opening and customer onboarding, which means faster revenue for banks and better experiences for customers. This isn't a nice-to-have; it's infrastructure that makes the financial system more accessible.

whyCompany.title: Y Combinator backing and strong founder signal clarity
whyCompany.body: Heang Chan built this at Stanford and has assembled a team trusted by major U.S. banks. Prelim is in the growth phase where a great Growth Marketing Associate can directly influence customer acquisition and retention. The role matters here.

whyCompany.title: My BDR and B2B digital background maps directly to this
whyCompany.body: At Copygen, I learned how to identify and reach paying customers through cold outreach and LinkedIn sequences. At NextAfter, I ran digital campaigns for B2B nonprofits and enterprises. Prelim's customers are banks and credit unions. I understand how to speak to them, build sequences, and think about conversion funnels in that world.

quote: I'm not chasing growth for growth's sake. I want to build growth strategy for a product that actually solves a problem in an industry that desperately needs modernization.
